title: List Reordering and Dynamic Lists
description: Add, remove, and reorder items with stable keys.

slides.title: Working with Dynamic Lists 🎛️
slides.content:
# Add, Remove, and Reorder

When lists change, keys must remain stable. Usually, your data items have unique `id` fields. If not, you can generate temporary IDs (e.g., using `uuid` or `crypto.randomUUID()`).

## Example: Adding and Removing

We'll maintain an array of objects with `id` and `text`.

slides.title: Todo List with Add/Remove
slides.content: Stable keys prevent bugs when manipulating the list:

quizData.question: Using `Date.now()` as a key is a good practice for production apps.
quizData.explanation: It's acceptable for simple demos, but in production you'd want a more robust unique ID generator (like `uuid`) to avoid collisions and ensure stability.
